moqui.agent.AgentUniversalPattern.chapterTitle: Setting Up Roles: What Parties Do
moqui.agent.AgentUniversalPattern.description: Volume 3 Chapter 2 covers declarative roles as reusable definitions of what a party may be or do.
moqui.agent.AgentUniversalPattern.moquiRelevance: Maps strongly to Party, PartyRole, RoleType, role groups, and role semantics used by service parameters and authorization decisions.

moqui.agent.AgentUniversalPatternLevel.moquiMappingNotes: Use when only role definitions are needed, such as RoleType and simple PartyRole linkage.

moqui.agent.AgentUniversalPatternLevel.structureFocus: Role type plus richer descriptive structure
moqui.agent.AgentUniversalPatternLevel.moquiMappingNotes: Useful when role definitions need attributes, grouping, or inheritance across role types.
Useful when the agent must reason over role metadata instead of just assigning hardcoded role ids.

moqui.agent.AgentUniversalPattern.chapterTitle: Using Roles: How Parties Are Involved
moqui.agent.AgentUniversalPattern.coreQuestion: How parties participate in a context
moqui.agent.AgentUniversalPattern.description: Volume 3 Chapter 3 extends roles into contextual participation patterns, where a party takes a role relative to another object or transaction.
moqui.agent.AgentUniversalPattern.moquiRelevance: Maps to WorkEffortParty, RequestParty, Order roles, customer/vendor roles, assignee roles, and any agent workflow that binds a party to a record with role semantics.

moqui.agent.AgentUniversalPatternLevel.structureFocus: Role participation with attributes and relationships
moqui.agent.AgentUniversalPatternLevel.moquiMappingNotes: Use for assignment records like WorkEffortParty or RequestParty where the relation itself has semantics.
Important when the same party can play multiple roles in the same domain object.
Useful for richer orchestration and RAG explanations of who is reporter, assignee, manager, customer, vendor, or owner.
Use when Moqui mixes explicit contextual records with simpler party references on the root entity.

moqui.agent.AgentUniversalPattern.description: Volume 3 Chapter 4 covers recursive structures, hierarchies, aggregations, and peer-to-peer organization of similar data.
moqui.agent.AgentUniversalPattern.moquiRelevance: This is the central chapter for WorkEffort, Facility, RequestItem, OrderPart/OrderItem, BudgetItem, and agent aggregate orchestration.

moqui.agent.AgentUniversalPatternLevel.moquiMappingNotes: Maps well to Facility parentFacilityId trees.
Maps to cases where the child still clearly belongs to a root, such as RequestItem under Request.
Maps to multilevel aggregates such as Budget -> BudgetItem -> BudgetItemDetail.
Maps directly to WorkEffort root plus parent trees.

moqui.agent.AgentUniversalPatternLevel.structureFocus: Recursive hierarchy with explicit business constraints
moqui.agent.AgentUniversalPatternLevel.moquiMappingNotes: Relevant when status, sequencing, or allowed child types must be validated by the agent.

moqui.agent.AgentUniversalPattern.chapterTitle: Types and Categories: the Classification of Data
moqui.agent.AgentUniversalPattern.description: Volume 3 Chapter 5 covers types, categorizations, and taxonomies.
moqui.agent.AgentUniversalPattern.moquiRelevance: Maps to Moqui Enumeration, type entities, status types, role groups, work effort purpose, order item types, and many other classification structures used in prompts and retrieval.

moqui.agent.AgentUniversalPatternLevel.moquiMappingNotes: Use for direct type fields like workEffortTypeEnumId or requestTypeEnumId.
Use when types are shared across multiple entities or need metadata.
Use for deeper agent reasoning over product, party, work effort, or accounting classifications.

moqui.agent.AgentUniversalPatternLevel.levelTitle: Level 3 Classification Pattern with Rollups and Schemes
moqui.agent.AgentUniversalPatternLevel.structureFocus: Classification with rollups and multiple schemes
moqui.agent.AgentUniversalPatternLevel.moquiMappingNotes: Important for Moqui enumerations, category schemes, and cases where the same record is classified in more than one hierarchy.

moqui.agent.AgentUniversalPattern.coreQuestion: What state a record is in
moqui.agent.AgentUniversalPattern.description: Volume 3 Chapter 6 covers status and state patterns.
moqui.agent.AgentUniversalPattern.moquiRelevance: Directly maps to Moqui StatusItem usage across requests, work efforts, orders, assignments, and lifecycle transitions that the agent must respect.

moqui.agent.AgentUniversalPatternLevel.moquiMappingNotes: Common in Moqui root entities with a direct statusId.
Relevant when current status is explicit but transitions are still important.
Use when the agent must reason about status progression or previous states.
Useful for advanced action guards and state-machine-like prompts.
Useful for broad policy checks such as open/closed/cancelled classes of state.

moqui.agent.AgentUniversalPattern.chapterTitle: Contact Mechanisms: How to Get in Touch
moqui.agent.AgentUniversalPattern.description: Volume 3 Chapter 7 covers contact mechanism modeling.
moqui.agent.AgentUniversalPattern.moquiRelevance: Maps to Moqui Party contact structures, addresses, telecoms, emails, facilities, and user-facing prompts that involve delivery, communication, and location data.

moqui.agent.AgentUniversalPatternLevel.moquiMappingNotes: Use for a single direct contact mechanism record.
Useful for PartyContactMech and similar linking structures.
Use when contact data has purpose, status, or lifecycle semantics.
Use for flexible addressing and complex communication preferences.

moqui.agent.AgentUniversalPatternLevel.levelTitle: Contact Mechanism Pattern with Geographic Boundary
moqui.agent.AgentUniversalPatternLevel.moquiMappingNotes: Relevant for addresses, facilities, shipping, and geography-aware routing.

moqui.agent.AgentUniversalPattern.chapterTitle: Business Rules: How Things Should Work
moqui.agent.AgentUniversalPattern.description: Volume 3 Chapter 8 covers business rules and how to model them explicitly.
moqui.agent.AgentUniversalPattern.moquiRelevance: Useful for guarded execution, action gating, status constraints, sequencing limits, allowed child structures, and policy-driven agent behavior.

moqui.agent.AgentUniversalPatternLevel.moquiMappingNotes: Use for explicit rule metadata instead of burying all policy in imperative code.
Useful when multiple entities, statuses, or role contexts affect execution.

moqui.agent.AgentUniversalPatternLevel.structureFocus: Rules constrained by party role participation
moqui.agent.AgentUniversalPatternLevel.moquiMappingNotes: Important when agent permissions or workflow steps depend on who is manager, assignee, customer, or owner.
